# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What This Project Is

**ATLAS** (`getatlas.ca`) is an AI-powered travel planning PWA. Users describe a trip and receive a structured itinerary, visa info, hotel picks, and an interactive map — generated by Claude, Groq, or Gemini depending on request complexity and user tier.

## Development Commands

This project has **no build step and no test suite**. Local development requires the Vercel CLI:

```bash
npm i -g vercel
vercel dev          # starts local dev server with API functions
```

API functions are plain ES module files — they can be tested by sending HTTP requests directly to `http://localhost:3000/api/<name>`.

To deploy:
```bash
vercel --prod
```

## Architecture

### No Bundler, No Framework

- `index.html` (~207 KB) contains the **entire frontend** as a single file: React 18 (CDN), Babel standalone (in-browser JSX transpilation), all styles, and all component code. There is no separate `src/` directory.
- `api/*.js` are **Vercel serverless functions** (ES modules, `"type": "module"` in package.json).
- Changes are deployed directly — no compilation.

### Routing (vercel.json)

All routing is defined in `vercel.json`:
- `/api/*` → serverless functions
- `/plan/:dest` → `api/plan.js` (SSG-style destination landing pages)
- `/blog/:slug` → `api/blog.js` (server-rendered HTML)
- `/admin` → `api/admin.js` (server-rendered HTML admin panel)
- `/trip/*` → `trip.html` (shareable saved-trip page)
- Everything else → `index.html` (SPA)

### AI Routing Strategy (api/chat.js)

The chat endpoint selects the AI model using this cascade:

1. **Destination-only detection** — if the user just named a destination with no budget/dates, return scripted questions immediately via SSE (no AI call at all).
2. **Groq (Llama-4-Scout)** — used for simple/early-conversation requests when `GROQ_API_KEY` is set.
3. **Gemini 2.0 Flash** — fallback when `GEMINI_API_KEY` is set and Groq fails/is skipped.
4. **Claude (claude-sonnet-4-20250514)** — used for multi-turn conversations, detailed requests, image inputs, and all Pro/Explorer users.

Rate limiting is **in-memory** (a `Map` keyed by user ID or IP), so limits reset on serverless cold-starts and do not persist across deployments.

### Subscription Tiers

| Tier | Plans/day | Tokens/day | AI model |
|------|-----------|------------|----------|
| guest | 3 | 100K | Cascading |
| free | 7 | 300K | Cascading |
| pro | 350 | 500K | Always Claude |
| explorer | 999 | 999K | Always Claude + custom prompt |

`allowed_users` table in Supabase grants `explorer` tier to whitelisted emails.

### Map Data Protocol

The AI system prompt instructs Claude to embed map data at the end of every trip plan in this exact format:

```
[MAPDATA]{"places":[{"name":"...", "lat":0.0, "lon":0.0, "day":1, "type":"city"}]}[/MAPDATA]
```

The frontend parses this to render an interactive Google Maps overlay. Do not change this format.

### Admin Panel (api/admin.js)

Single-file, server-rendered HTML admin at `/admin`. Cookie-authenticated via SHA256-hashed password. Sections: Overview, Blog Articles, Schedule, Community Posts. Publishing a blog article triggers:
1. Web push notifications to all `push_subscriptions` subscribers
2. Newsletter emails to `newsletter_subscribers` via Zoho SMTP

### Blog Content System

Blog posts are stored in Supabase (`blog_posts` table) as raw HTML. Inline photos use placeholder syntax in content: `[photo-1]`, `[photo-2]`, … `[photo-5]` — these are replaced at render time with URLs from the `inline_photos` JSONB array column. Photos are uploaded to Supabase Storage (`blog-images` bucket).

### Service Workers

Two service workers exist:
- `service-worker.js` (cache name `atlas-v5`) — handles static asset caching with network-first strategy; API calls bypass cache entirely.
- `sw.js` (cache name `atlas-v1`) — handles push notification display and notification click routing.

## Supabase Schema (inferred)

Tables used across `api/*.js`:
- `allowed_users` — whitelisted explorer users (`email`, `created_at`, `plan`)
- `subscriptions` — Stripe subscriptions (`user_id`, `stripe_customer_id`, `stripe_subscription_id`, `plan`, `status`, `current_period_end`)
- `saved_plans` — user-saved itineraries (`user_id`, `user_email`, `title`, `destination`, `is_public`, `created_at`)
- `blog_posts` — (`slug`, `title`, `description`, `category`, `read_time`, `is_published`, `date_published`, `cover_image_url`, `content`, `inline_photos`)
- `push_subscriptions` — web push endpoints (`user_id`, `subscription` JSON string)
- `newsletter_subscribers` — (`email`, `source`, `unsubscribed`, `subscribed_at`)
- `user_posts` — community submissions (`id`, `user_name`, `user_email`, `title`, `destination`, `excerpt`, `content`, `cover_photo`, `status`, `created_at`)
- `api_usage_log` — Claude token tracking (`user_id`, `model`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cost_usd`)

Supabase URL is hardcoded in several files: `https://prffhhkemxibujjjiyhg.supabase.co`.

## Environment Variables

**Required for core functionality:**
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` — service role key (used server-side only)
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — Claude API
- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` + `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — payments
- `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY` — server-side: Geocoding, Places v1, Routes, Weather APIs
- `GOOGLE_JAVASCRIPT_API_KEY` — served to frontend via `/api/map-config` for the Maps JS SDK
- `VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY` — web push (public key is hardcoded in `api/admin.js`)
- `ZOHO_FROM` + `ZOHO_PASS` — transactional email (Zoho SMTP)

**Optional (enable additional features):**
- `GROQ_API_KEY` — enables Llama-4 routing for simple requests
- `GEMINI_API_KEY` — enables Gemini fallback
- `TAVILY_API_KEY` — real-time web search for visa/travel data
- `YOUTUBE_API_KEY` — travel video search (falls back to YouTube search URL)
- `ZOHO_APP_PASSWORD` — separate credential used in `api/admin.js` for newsletter emails
- `ADMIN_PASSWORD` — admin panel password (defaults to a hardcoded value if unset)

## Stripe Plan IDs

Hardcoded in `api/create-checkout.js` — update here if plans change in the Stripe dashboard:
- `pro_monthly`: `price_1TGqLxB0HqRlFQjUckzzRyed`
- `pro_yearly`: `price_1TGqLvB0HqRlFQjUnkJMKhOH`
- `explorer_monthly`: `price_1TGqLzB0HqRlFQjUz54ahjDk`
- `explorer_yearly`: `price_1TGqLyB0HqRlFQjUr9Gr0Sup`

## SEO Destination Pages (api/plan.js)

Static destination data (highlights, FAQs, visa info, budgets) for ~25 cities is hardcoded in `api/plan.js` as a `DESTINATIONS` object. Each entry renders a full SEO-optimised HTML page with JSON-LD structured data. Unknown slugs redirect to `/`.

## Cron Job

`api/cron-report.js` runs daily at 09:00 via Vercel cron (configured in `vercel.json`) and emails a metrics summary (active users, trips created, new subscriptions, AI costs) to `ZOHO_FROM`.

## Key Conventions

- All API handlers export a default `async function handler(req, res)` — standard Vercel serverless function signature.
- SSE streaming: `api/chat.js` streams tokens via `text/event-stream` with `data: {"type":"delta","text":"..."}` events and a final `data: {"type":"done","plansLeft":N}` event.
- The `x-user-prefs` request header carries base64+URI-encoded JSON with user preferences (passport, home city, travel style, language, currency, custom prompt).
- User tier and token counts live in the in-memory `userMap` — they are not persisted to the database (only Claude usage is logged to `api_usage_log`).
- The frontend reads the Google Maps API key at runtime from `/api/map-config` before loading the Maps JS SDK dynamically.
